prompt:
I want to create a new standalone notifications microservice from scratch. Here are the answers to all the setup questions:

1. Domain: notifications
2. Entity: Notification
3. Fields: id (uuid), userId (string), deviceToken (string), title (string), body (string), type ('push'), status ('pending' | 'sent' | 'failed'), metadata (optional Record<string,unknown>), createdAt (Date), updatedAt (Date)
4. States: pending → sent via markAsSent(), pending → failed via markAsFailed(reason: string) — terminal states cannot transition further
5. Use cases: create-notification, get-notification, mark-as-sent, mark-as-failed
6. Inbound: two adapters — Kafka consumer (primary: listens to events from other services like 'order.created' and creates Notification entities) AND Express HTTP (for internal status queries)
7. Write DB: Postgres (Prisma)
8. Read DB: same as write, no CQRS
9. External: FCM (Firebase Cloud Messaging) outbound push adapter — INotificationPushPort abstract class + FcmPushAdapter; create-notification use case calls push.send(deviceToken, title, body) after persisting
10. Monorepo: standalone, create from scratch
11. TDD: yes — write failing tests for each layer before implementing it (Red → Green → Refactor per layer)

Please scaffold the complete service following every step of the hexagonal microservice skill, including all layers, tests (TDD-first), and the quality checklist.

assertions.text: domain-no-imports: notification.ts has zero imports from external frameworks or libraries (no express, prisma, kafkajs, nestjs, ioredis)
ports-are-abstract-classes: all port definitions use 'abstract class' keyword, not 'interface'
status-as-const: Notification status is defined as an 'as const' object, not a TypeScript enum
private-constructor: Notification class uses a private constructor (forces use of create() and reconstitute())
terminal-state-guard: markAsSent() and markAsFailed() throw when the notification is already in a terminal state (sent or failed)
use-cases-wrap-telemetry-span: every use case's execute() body is wrapped in this.telemetry.span(...)
fcm-push-port-exists: INotificationPushPort (or equivalent) abstract class exists as an outbound port
fcm-adapter-implements-port: FcmPushAdapter (or equivalent) extends the push port abstract class
kafka-consumer-inbound: a Kafka consumer adapter exists under adapters/inbound/messaging/kafka/ (not outbound)
kafka-consumer-separate-bootstrap: the consumer has its own bootstrap.ts separate from the Express HTTP bootstrap
kafka-topic-naming: Kafka topics follow notification.<past-tense-verb> convention (e.g. notification.sent, notification.failed)
consumer-no-business-logic: the Kafka consumer adapter only parses messages and delegates to use cases — no domain logic inline
redis-cache-ttl: the Redis cache adapter's set() call includes a TTL (EX or PX parameter)
config-zod-validated: infrastructure/config.ts uses a Zod schema to parse and validate environment variables
js-extensions-on-imports: all local imports use the .js extension (required for NodeNext module resolution)
test-doubles-extend-abstract-ports: doubles.ts uses 'extends' on the abstract port classes, not vi.fn() or plain object literals
e2e-uses-in-memory-adapters: _test/e2e/setup.ts wires the real controller with in-memory test doubles — no real DB or Kafka
tdd-test-files-exist: test files exist for every layer (domain/_test/, use-cases/_test/, _test/e2e/)
no-process-env-outside-config: process.env is only accessed inside infrastructure/config.ts, not in adapters, use cases, or domain
graceful-shutdown-allsettled: Express bootstrap uses Promise.allSettled to close multiple connections on shutdown
